---
title: "deform_to_sdf"
generated: true
description: "Advance a mesh's vertices along their normals onto a target SDF zero-set."
---

[â€ą back to `nitrix.geometry.surface`](../nitrix-geometry-surface.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:deform_to_sdf -->
```python
deform_to_sdf(mesh: Mesh, sdf: Float[Array, 'x y z'], *, n_iterations: int = 50, step: float = 0.2, smooth_weight: float = 0.1, spacing: Tuple[float, float, float] = (1.0, 1.0, 1.0), mode: BoundaryMode = 'nearest', cval: float = 0.0) -> Mesh
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:deform_to_sdf -->
Advance a mesh's vertices along their normals onto a target SDF zero-set.

The recommended *geometry-light* surface mover: each iteration samples the
signed-distance field at the current vertices, steps every vertex a
fraction `step` of its local SDF value back along its (outward) normal
-- $v \leftarrow v - \mathrm{step} \cdot \mathrm{sdf}(v) \cdot n$
-- then applies a Laplacian-smoothing fraction.  Deforming e.g. a white
surface outward onto a pial SDF this way **preserves vertex correspondence
and inherits genus-0** (the topology / `faces` never change -- no
re-tessellation, no topology correction), and correspondence cortical
thickness falls out for free.

A jitted `lax.fori_loop` of pure-JAX ops ([`sample_at_points`](../nitrix-geometry-grid/sample_at_points.qmd) +
`compute_vertex_normals` + `mesh_laplacian_smooth`), so it is
**fully differentiable** with respect to both the `sdf` and the initial
vertices -- usable inside a learned-refinement loss.  Regularisation is
in-loop and jittable only: no host-side self-intersection guard (run
[`remove_self_intersections`](../nitrix-geometry-intersection/remove_self_intersections.qmd) as a post-hoc cleanup if needed).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:deform_to_sdf -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `mesh` | `Mesh` | â€” | â€” | Genus-0 starting mesh (e.g. a white surface). |
| `sdf` | `Float` | `x` Ă— `y` Ă— `z` | â€” | Target signed-distance volume `(X, Y, Z)` (negative inside), in the same physical frame as the mesh (sampled at `vertices / spacing`). |
| `n_iterations` | `int` | â€” | `50` | Number of march iterations (static). |
| `step` | `float` | â€” | `0.2` | Per-iteration fraction of the local SDF value to move (`0 < step <= 1`); smaller is more stable. |
| `smooth_weight` | `float` | â€” | `0.1` | Laplacian-smoothing fraction applied each iteration (`0` = none). |
| `spacing` | `Tuple[float, float, float]` | â€” | `(1.0, 1.0, 1.0)` | Per-axis voxel size relating vertex physical coordinates to `sdf` index space. |
| `mode` | `BoundaryMode` | â€” | `'nearest'` | Out-of-bounds boundary mode for the SDF sample (default `'nearest'` edge-clamp -- sensible for marching, so a vertex stepping outside the volume reads the border distance rather than a zero-fill cliff). This is the *boundary* mode; the interpolation kernel stays `Linear` (so the differentiability claim holds). |
| `cval` | `float` | â€” | `0.0` | Fill value for `mode='constant'`. |

**Returns** `Mesh`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:deform_to_sdf -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `x` | *(undocumented â€” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `y` | *(undocumented â€” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `z` | *(undocumented â€” add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
